projectDescription: Voice AI observability platform that indexes acoustic features of phone calls — tone, prosody, and frustration — making conversations searchable and debuggable at scale.
keyFeatures: Natural language corpus search over voice calls
Acoustic feature indexing: tone, prosody, tension, rhythm, intent
Turn-level call replay with audio playback
Defect signature clustering across thousands of calls
MCP server for AI-native debugging workflows
Real-time signal detection during live calls
Integrations with LiveKit, Twilio, Telnyx, Pipecat, and Datadog
Structured, machine-readable output for LLM-assisted root cause analysis
publicApiSurface: voice.query() — natural language search over call corpus
Corpus search results with defect signatures and cluster groupings
Turn-level audio S3 keys and replay bundles
MCP server endpoints: corpus search, turn lookup, replay, defect signatures
Call data model: calls, transcript_turns, tool_calls, system_events
Acoustic feature dimensions: tone, prosody, tension, rhythm, intent
Defect signature schema for LLM-readable structured output
